stdout:
Research summary: fully static GitHub Pages cannot process form submissions itself, so every viable option uses either a hosted form endpoint, GitHub as the moderation database, or a host with built-in form handling. I did not find strong GitHub Pages classifieds examples with anonymous public self-posting; the common static-site pattern is “submit elsewhere, moderate, then rebuild static JSON/HTML.”

Sources checked:
GitHub Pages: https://pages.github.com/
GitHub Pages form limitation discussion: https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/44845
Formspree: https://formspree.io/ and https://help.formspree.io/articles/troubleshooting/how-to-prevent-spam/
Web3Forms: https://web3forms.com/ and https://docs.web3forms.com/getting-started/customizations/spam-protection
Web3Forms pricing: https://web3forms.com/pricing
WhatsApp click-to-chat: https://faq.whatsapp.com/425247423114725
Netlify Forms docs: https://docs.netlify.com/manage/forms/usage-and-billing/

Top 3 recommended approaches:

1. WhatsApp-first static form using Web3Forms, manual approval into classifieds.json

Best fit for Paradisio.

Tech stack:
Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS form on GitHub Pages.
Web3Forms as the form endpoint.
WhatsApp contact field as primary identity/contact method.
Optional email field, not public.
Existing Python build pipeline continues to read classifieds.json.

Setup:
Create a “Publicar anuncio” page.
Fields: category, title, description, price, neighborhood, contact WhatsApp, optional email, expiration date, language, consent checkbox.
POST the form to Web3Forms.
Use a hidden subject like “Nuevo clasificado Paradisio”.
Redirect successful submissions to a static gracias.html page.
Add a visible WhatsApp fallback button using wa.me with prefilled text:
https://wa.me/YOURNUMBER?text=<urlencoded classified draft>
Keep public listings showing only the poster’s preferred contact method, ideally a wa.me link or masked phone display.

Moderation workflow:
Submission arrives by email.
You review it.
If approved, copy or script-import it into classifieds.json.
Run existing build and push.
Rejected posts are ignored or replied to manually.
For a small improvement, save email submissions as .json drafts locally and write a small Python importer that validates required fields before appending.

Spam resistance:
Web3Forms runs server-side spam checks.
Enable Cloudflare Turnstile or reCAPTCHA if needed.
Restrict allowed domains in Web3Forms.
Add a honeypot field, but do not rely on it alone.
Require WhatsApp number and category.
Add client-side minimum text length and a simple “Puerto Viejo area only” checkbox.
At 1-5 posts/week, manual moderation is the strongest filter.

Annual cost:
Likely $0.
Web3Forms has a free tier suitable for low volume. Paid plans only become relevant if you want higher limits or advanced features.

Tradeoffs:
Simplest and most local-friendly.
No account required for posters.
No backend.
Still requires manual approval and data entry unless you add a local importer.
Submissions live in a third-party inbox/service, not directly in GitHub.


2. GitHub Issues as the moderation queue, GitHub Actions publishes approved posts

Best if you want submissions to become structured repo artifacts with less copy-paste.

Tech stack:
GitHub Issue Forms or issue templates.
GitHub Actions.
Python script that reads approved issues via GitHub API and generates classifieds.json or a pending JSON file.
GitHub Pages for hosting.

Setup:
Create issue templates for categories: room, job, gig, for sale, service, event, rideshare.
Required fields match classifieds.json.
Add labels: classified-submission, pending, approved, rejected, published.
Create a GitHub Action triggered manually or on issue label change.
The action reads issues labeled approved and not published.
It converts issue body fields into normalized JSON.
It opens a PR or commits to a generated file such as paradisio_app/classifieds/classifieds.generated.json.
After publish, it adds published label or comments with the generated listing ID.

Moderation workflow:
Poster opens a GitHub issue.
You review the issue.
You apply approved label.
Action rebuilds static data and deploys Pages.
Rejected issues get rejected label and are closed.
Published listings can be updated by editing the issue and rerunning the action.

Spam resistance:
GitHub account requirement blocks most anonymous bot spam.
GitHub has its own abuse controls.
Issue templates force structured fields.
Labels prevent auto-publishing.
Can add GitHub Actions validation: reject missing WhatsApp, overlong descriptions, too many links, banned words.

Annual cost:
$0 for a public repo within normal GitHub Actions/Page usage.

Tradeoffs:
Not good for Puerto Viejo locals because posters need GitHub accounts.
Not truly anonymous to you or to GitHub; public issues can expose usernames and any contact details the user enters.
You can avoid exposing email by never asking for it, but WhatsApp numbers in issues may be public unless you use a private repo or a separate intake path.
Technically elegant, socially wrong for many phone-first users.

Recommendation:
Use this only as an internal moderation CMS, not as the public submission path, unless the submission repo is private and you are comfortable with the GitHub account barrier.


3. Move classifieds posting surface to Netlify Forms, keep GitHub as source of truth

Best if you are willing to deploy the static site through Netlify instead of only GitHub Pages.

Tech stack:
Same vanilla static site.
Netlify hosting connected to the GitHub repo.
Netlify Forms for submission capture.
Optional Netlify spam filtering/honeypot.
Existing Python build pipeline still commits classifieds.json to GitHub.

Setup:
Connect the GitHub repo to Netlify.
Add a static HTML form with netlify attribute and a hidden form-name field.
Add honeypot field using netlify-honeypot.
Configure email notifications for new submissions.
Keep GitHub Pages as a backup/mirror if desired, but form processing only works reliably on Netlify’s deployed site.
Moderation still writes to classifieds.json and rebuilds.

Moderation workflow:
Submission lands in Netlify Forms dashboard and/or email.
You review.
Approved posts are imported manually or via CSV export into classifieds.json.
Push to GitHub.
Netlify rebuilds automatically.
Rejected posts stay unpublished.

Spam resistance:
Netlify Forms supports built-in form handling and spam controls.
Use honeypot, required WhatsApp, max links, category validation, and manual approval.
Because no post goes live automatically, spam mostly costs review time, not public damage.

Annual cost:
Possibly $0 at very low volume, depending on the current Netlify plan and forms billing.
Verify current billing before relying on it; Netlify’s form pricing/limits have changed over time and current docs describe forms billing by plan/credits.

Tradeoffs:
Very clean static-site form workflow.
Better dashboard than raw email.
But it violates the strict “GitHub Pages only” constraint if the production form must work on GitHub Pages.
If GitHub Pages remains the canonical public URL, Netlify Forms is not the right primary choice.

Final recommendation:
Implement option 1 first: Web3Forms plus WhatsApp-first fields, manual moderation, and a small local importer. It best matches the local user behavior, keeps GitHub Pages, costs nothing at expected volume, and avoids exposing poster email publicly. Option 2 is useful later as an internal publishing automation pattern. Option 3 is strong only if you are open to switching hosting from GitHub Pages to Netlify.

# Request for Solutions — Anonymous Classifieds Posting for Static Site

## Context

Paradisio has a classifieds board (rooms, jobs, gigs, for sale, services, events, rideshare) at `paradisio_app/classifieds/`. It's a static site on GitHub Pages — no server, no database, no backend.

Currently, users "post" via a mailto: link that opens their email client. The email goes to `paradisio@example.com`. I manually copy the ad into `classifieds.json`, rebuild, and push. This doesn't scale.

## Requirements

1. **Poster anonymity**: Poster's real email should not be exposed on the public listing page
2. **Spam resistance**: Must have some barrier to prevent automated spam posts
3. **Moderation**: I must review posts before they go live (no auto-publish)
4. **Zero infrastructure**: No server, no database — GitHub Pages only
5. **Low cost**: Ideally free, or very low cost
6. **Puerto Viejo context**: Many posters may not have email but have WhatsApp. The flow should match how locals actually communicate

## Constraints

- Static site on GitHub Pages (no server-side code)
- Current stack: Python stdlib build pipeline, vanilla HTML/CSS/JS
- Poster demographic: mostly Spanish-speaking, phone-first, WhatsApp-native
- Volume: low (maybe 1-5 posts per week)

## Research Questions

1. What approaches do other static-site classifieds boards use for posting?
2. What third-party form services work with static sites and support moderation queues?
3. Can WhatsApp serve as the primary contact layer instead of email?
4. Are there lightweight issue-tracker-as-CMS patterns that work for this?
5. What's the simplest possible approach that isn't manual copy-paste?

Please research and recommend the top 3 approaches with tradeoffs.


web search: 
web search: GitHub Pages classifieds board posting static site GitHub issues
web search: 
web search: GitHub Issues CMS static site submissions GitHub Actions
web search: 
web search: Formspree pricing free submissions 2026
web search: 
web search: github classifieds static site repository
